---
title: "Emotional avoidance 2"
format: html
---


# Choosing Your Next Step

Recovery is not about forcing yourself into difficult situations. It’s about making intentional choices.

You can either:

- Continue avoiding (for safety and stability), or  
- Begin gradual, supported re-engagement  

Both paths are valid depending on your readiness.

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/startingline.jpg?raw=true){fig-align="center"}


## Use this worksheet to decide your next step with a specific emotionally triggering place.


### 1. Readiness Check

How ready do you feel to face this place?

- 0 (not ready) → 10 (ready): _____  

Why this number?



### 2. Choose Your Path

#### Option A: Intentional Avoidance

If you are not ready:

- [ ] I will continue avoiding this place  
- [ ] I will do so without guilt  
- [ ] I will revisit this later  

Supportive alternative activity:



#### Option B: Small Step Re-Engagement

If you are somewhat ready, choose one:

- [ ] Think about the place  
- [ ] Look at photos/maps  
- [ ] Visit briefly  
- [ ] Go with support  
- [ ] Go during a low-risk time  



### 3. Plan Ahead (If Re-Engaging)

- **What will help me cope?**  

- **When will I leave?**  


### 4. Exit Permission

Complete this sentence:

> “If I feel overwhelmed, I will __________________ immediately.”


### 5. Confidence Check

How confident do you feel about your plan (0–10)? _____
